lens: integration QA pass-2 under 'Verify, don't trust docs' discipline — walk QUICKSTART for real, sample passing tests for vacuity, re-check pass-1 findings
headline: Pass-1's r6-qa-1 (stale ignore text) IS GENUINELY FIXED — all 10 ignore markers now name the TRUE Phase-2 blocker per-test. Pass-1 findings r6-qa-2/3/6 remain open as scoped. But walking QUICKSTART.md verbatim exposed FOUR previously-uncaught doc-vs-code drifts that break the 10-minute path at the spot a new user would first hit: (1) `postHandlers.create.toMermaid()` — `.create` is undefined, `crud('post')` returns {subgraph, actions, label, stampCreatedAt}. (2) `engine.trace('post:create', {...})` — shipped signature is 3-arg `trace(handlerId, op, input)`; 2-arg call throws 'Failed to convert JavaScript value into rust type String' with code E_UNKNOWN. (3) `engine.call('post:create', {...})` — shipped signature needs handlerId first; 2-arg throws E_DSL_UNREGISTERED_HANDLER. (4) `crud('post', { schema: {...} })` — `schema` is NOT in `CrudOptions` (only label/hlc/capability); option is silently accepted via TS option-object laxity and dropped. A user following the docs verbatim gets three confusing errors and one silent-no-op. The CAPABILITY section of QUICKSTART DOES work end-to-end when spelled correctly via `Engine.openWithPolicy(..., PolicyKind.GrantBacked) + grantCapability + call(handlerId, op, input)`. Also: SECURITY-POSTURE.md is missing the Compromise #8 section that commit 25d2893 should have added.

methodology_applied.doctrine: Verify, don't trust docs — run the actual code paths and measure, do not read claims in markdown and mark closed
how: Scaffolded a fresh project via `npx create-benten-app qa-verify-app` into /tmp — actually ran it
Ran `npm install` + `npm run test` + `npm run build` + `npm run dev` against the generated project
Copy-pasted QUICKSTART.md code snippets verbatim into fresh test files inside the scaffold and executed them
Re-verified r6-qa-1 fix by grepping ALL 17 #[ignore] markers in the Rust crates and checking each reason against the actual grant_backed.rs impl
Sampled 10 Rust test files (across benten-caps/core/engine/eval/errors) for vacuous assertions — every one exercised real state mutations with observable diffs
Read primitive_host.rs to verify r6-qa-2 (fallback-branch silent bypass) — still present, still dormant
Read outcome.rs HandlerPredecessors to verify r6-qa-3 (topo-order vacuous) — stub still returns &[] empty slice
Read docs/SECURITY-POSTURE.md top-to-bottom to verify r6-qa-4 — Compromise #8 section STILL missing
methodology_applied.what_pass_1_missed: Pass-1 read QUICKSTART.md but did not execute its code snippets against the shipped @benten/engine. Four doc-vs-code drifts slipped through. Pass-1 also did not verify SECURITY-POSTURE.md #8 closure — the commit message at 25d2893 claimed closure but the doc section was never added.

The two files pass-1 flagged that no longer carry ignore markers at all — cap_toctou.rs and ucan_stub_messages.rs — were rewritten at r6-sec-2 to NOT need the stub. cap_toctou.rs now has a real regression test (line 94 `capability_revocation_at_batch_boundary_surfaces_mid_eval_code`) that uses a RevokeAfterNPolicy wrapper to synthesize the scheduled revocation without depending on the Phase-2 API. Genuinely stronger than a stub.

quickstart_walk_through.summary: The 10-minute DX path works when you spell the API CORRECTLY. But the QUICKSTART doc shows FOUR incorrect spellings that a user following it verbatim will hit.
installed_works: `npx create-benten-app qa-verify-app --skip-install` scaffolds {README.md, package.json, src/{handlers.ts, index.ts}, test/smoke.test.ts, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts} — works
`npm run test` — 6 tests pass in 73ms inside the scaffold — works
`npm run dev` — prints `registered handler: crud:post` and `actions: create, get, list, update, delete` — works
works_when_spelled_via_actual_api_not_do: Capability enforcement: `Engine.openWithPolicy(path, PolicyKind.GrantBacked)` + `crud('post', { capability: 'store:post:*' })` + write-without-grant → E_CAP_DENIED + `grantCapability({actor, scope: 'store:post:write'})` → write succeeds. Verified end-to-end with 2 fresh tests
Trace works with 3-arg `engine.trace(handlerId, op, input)` — returns steps with {nodeCid, primitive, durationUs, inputs, outputs, error}. Real per-step timings (46us seen)

Top-level `registered.toMermaid()` works — returns `flowchart TD
    crud_post_write(["WRITE: crud_post_write"])
    respond_1(["RESPOND: respond_1"])
    crud_post_write --> respond_1`. That IS the real subgraph's shape

doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.ship_truth: crud('post') returns { subgraph, actions, label, stampCreatedAt } — NO `.create` property. After registerSubgraph(), the registered handle has { id, actions, subgraph, toMermaid } — still no per-action `.create` object. `postHandlers.create` is `undefined`; `.create.toMermaid()` throws TypeError
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.verification: Ran `expect(h.create).toBeDefined()` against scaffolded project — AssertionError: expected undefined to be defined

doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.doc_snippet: const trace = await engine.trace('post:create', { title: 'Test', body: 'Trace me' });
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.ship_truth: engine.trace is 3-arg: `trace(handlerId, op, input)`. The 2-arg call treats the second arg as `op` (string) and throws at the napi boundary
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.verification_output: threw: Failed to convert JavaScript value `Object {"title":"Test","body":"Trace me"}` into rust type `String` | code: E_UNKNOWN

doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.doc_snippet: await engine.call('post:create', { title: 'Hello Benten', body: 'First post.' }); ... await engine.call('post:list', { limit: 10 });
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.ship_truth: engine.call is 3-arg: `call(handlerId, op, input)`. The handler-id for `crud('post')` is `crud:post`. The 2-arg call treats `post:create` as the handlerId lookup key and can't find it
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.verification_output: threw: no handler 'post:create' registered; known handlers: crud:post | code: E_DSL_UNREGISTERED_HANDLER

doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.doc_snippet: crud('post', { schema: { title: { type: 'string', required: true, maxLength: 200 }, body: {...}, publishedAt: {...} } });
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.ship_truth: CrudOptions interface (packages/engine/src/dsl.ts:523-540) has ONLY { label, hlc, capability }. No `schema`. Passing it is silently accepted due to TS option-object laxity; a 500-char title with stated maxLength: 200 is ACCEPTED (CID returned)
doc_drifts_that_break_verbatim_copy_past.verification_output: 500-char title accepted — schema not enforced. CID: bafyr4idoln23tsyupfkxumay5hiirqercgvkme4g5uma556imnxkyrx2zq

stale_module_comment_finds: crates/benten-caps/tests/call_attenuation.rs:12 — 'Status: FAILING until R5 lands the attenuation check in the evaluator CALL dispatch.' Test passes. Attenuation is exercised via testing::check_attenuation helper (unit level), not via the evaluator CALL dispatch (which is Phase-2). The status line is stale
crates/benten-engine/tests/integration/ivm_propagation.rs:8 — '**Status:** FAILING until G3 + G5 + G7 land.' Test passes. Stale

crates/benten-engine/tests/engine_builder_thinness.rs:17-19 — 'R3 contract: Engine::builder() does not exist today (the spike's Engine is Engine::open(path) directly). R5 (G7-A) introduces EngineBuilder.' Stale — builder exists and tests use it successfully
These are low-severity narrative-rot; the code runs. Recommend a mechanical sed-pass to replace 'FAILING until...' with 'PASSING post-R5' or drop the status line

new_findings.claim: `postHandlers.create.toMermaid()` — the documented form — throws TypeError. `.create` does not exist on the crud() bundle or on the registered handler
new_findings.detail: crud('post') returns { subgraph, actions: string[], label, stampCreatedAt } — a flat object with no per-action sub-handle. After engine.registerSubgraph() the shape is { id, actions: string[], subgraph, toMermaid() }. There is no `.create` nested object on either. A user copy-pasting line 80 of QUICKSTART hits `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'toMermaid')`
new_findings.verification: Added `expect(h.create).toBeDefined()` to a fresh scaffold test — `AssertionError: expected undefined to be defined`
new_findings.suggested_fix: Change QUICKSTART line 80 to `console.log(registered.toMermaid())` where `registered = await engine.registerSubgraph(postHandlers)`. Alternative: add a per-action proxy on the registered handle that forwards `.create.toMermaid()` / `.list.toMermaid()` etc. Doc-fix is 3x cheaper than ship-fix

new_findings.claim: `engine.trace('post:create', {...})` — the documented 2-arg form — throws 'Failed to convert JavaScript value into rust type String' with code E_UNKNOWN. The shipped signature is 3-arg `trace(handlerId, op, input)`
new_findings.detail: packages/engine/src/engine.ts:523-527 declares `public async trace(handlerId: string, op: string, input: JsonValue): Promise<Trace>`. The 2-arg doc example maps the object literal into the `op: string` slot and napi fails to coerce at the boundary. The resulting error code E_UNKNOWN is NOT in the error catalog — it's the fallback mapNativeError produces for unrecognized shapes
new_findings.verification: Ran `(engine as any).trace('post:create', {...})` — threw 'Failed to convert JavaScript value Object {...} into rust type String' | code: E_UNKNOWN
new_findings.suggested_fix: Update QUICKSTART line 85 to `const trace = await engine.trace(handler.id, 'post:create', { title: 'Test', body: 'Trace me' });` — matching the shipped signature. Same pattern correction is needed at lines 44-47 for `engine.call`

new_findings.claim: `engine.call('post:create', { title: ..., body: ... })` — the documented 2-arg form — throws E_DSL_UNREGISTERED_HANDLER because the shipped signature is `call(handlerId, op, input)` and the handler-id for `crud('post')` is `crud:post`, not `post:create`
new_findings.detail: packages/engine/src/engine.ts declares `public async call(handlerId: string, op: string, input?: JsonValue)`. Calling with ('post:create', {...}) looks up a handler named 'post:create', finds nothing, throws. Same drift as r6b-qa-2 but for the main call path that EVERY user hits on step 3 of the 10-minute quickstart
new_findings.verification_output: threw: no handler 'post:create' registered; known handlers: crud:post | code: E_DSL_UNREGISTERED_HANDLER
new_findings.suggested_fix: QUICKSTART step 3 code block must show: `const handler = await engine.registerSubgraph(postHandlers); await engine.call(handler.id, 'post:create', { title: 'Hello Benten', body: 'First post.' }); const posts = await engine.call(handler.id, 'post:list', { limit: 10 });`. Without this, the FIRST THING a new user does fails with an error message that doesn't map to the doc at all

new_findings.claim: `crud('post', { schema: { title: { ..., maxLength: 200 }, body: ..., publishedAt: ... } })` — the documented 'Adding schema validation' section — is a silent no-op. `schema` is not in CrudOptions; a 500-char title with stated maxLength=200 is accepted
new_findings.detail: packages/engine/src/dsl.ts:523-540 CrudOptions = { label?, hlc?, capability? }. No schema. TypeScript wouldn't catch `schema` literal without `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` or strict checking. At runtime, crud() ignores unknown keys silently (no throw, no warning). Schema enforcement is Phase-2 per CLAUDE.md, but the QUICKSTART section documents it as Phase-1 usage
new_findings.verification: Ran `crud('post', { schema: {...} })` + created a post with 500-char title despite maxLength=200 — succeeded with a valid CID returned
new_findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) delete the 'Adding schema validation' section from QUICKSTART.md and restore it as a Phase-2 doc marked 'coming in Phase 2', OR (b) add a runtime throw for unknown CrudOptions keys so a user typo'ing `schema` gets a clear message instead of silent drop. Option (a) is the faster honest path

new_findings.claim: Compromise #8 (Engine::call CRUD fast-path bypassed evaluator) is missing from SECURITY-POSTURE.md despite commit 25d2893 'feat(benten-eval,benten-engine): PrimitiveHost trait — Engine::call dispatches through Evaluator' claiming closure. Pass-1 flagged this; pass-2 confirms no section was ever added
new_findings.detail: Pass-1 found r6-qa-4 'compromises #1, #3, #4, #5, #7, #8 missing'. Commits 90eb694 (BLAKE3 hash choice for #6), d03f642 (extract ErrorCode for #3), ffa2c5b (metrics for #5), de3f01b (TOCTOU for #1 + bin gating for #7) added the #1/#3/#5/#6/#7 sections (#2 and #4 were already present). #8 was NOT backfilled
new_findings.verification: `grep -n 'Compromise #8|compromise_8|Engine::call fast-path|CRUD fast-path' docs/SECURITY-POSTURE.md` returns zero matches. The doc ends with a boilerplate footer 'Future compromises with security implications will be appended as sections here, each tagged with the compromise number from the R1 Triage Addendum.' — no #8 appended
new_findings.suggested_fix: Append a ~15-line Compromise #8 section documenting the CRUD fast-path → PrimitiveHost→Evaluator refactor landed at 25d2893. Template mirrors the #7 CLOSED section shape

new_findings.claim: The scaffolded project's exit-criterion gate #3 was REDUCED from 'capability denial' to 'unregistered handler lookup'. The comment honestly names it but the substitution weakens the headline exit criterion — the 10-minute user never exercises the capability flow in their smoke test
new_findings.detail: Lines 54-69: 'the original Phase-1 gate exercised capability denial, but capability-gated crud() variants (with a capability: option) are Phase-2 DSL surface. Until that lands, we exercise the same typed-error-surface contract via an unregistered-handler lookup.' The capability FLOW works (openWithPolicy + grantCapability verified in this audit), but the template smoke test does not exercise it. New users who run `npm test` against their scaffold get a false sense that 'cap denial is covered' when really just 'some typed error surfaces with a code'. Conflicts with QUICKSTART line 56-61 which shows `crud('post', { capability: 'store:post:*' })` as the THIRD example
new_findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the template smoke gate-3 to exercise the capability path: open with PolicyKind.GrantBacked, register crud with capability option, try-call without grant, assert E_CAP_DENIED. This works today (I verified). The 'Phase-2 DSL surface' comment is stale — the surface IS in Phase 1

new_findings.claim: Three test files carry 'Status: FAILING until Rx lands...' module-doc headers that are stale — the tests now pass post-R5
detail: crates/benten-caps/tests/call_attenuation.rs:12 'Status: FAILING until R5 lands the attenuation check in the evaluator CALL dispatch.' — test passes (exercises testing::check_attenuation helper, not evaluator CALL)
crates/benten-engine/tests/integration/ivm_propagation.rs:8 '**Status:** FAILING until G3 + G5 + G7 land.' — test passes
crates/benten-engine/tests/engine_builder_thinness.rs:17-19 'R3 contract: Engine::builder() does not exist today...R5 (G7-A) introduces EngineBuilder.' — builder exists; tests use it
new_findings.suggested_fix: Mechanical sed-pass replacing 'FAILING until' with 'PASSING post-R5 — kept as a regression gate' and dropping the 'does not exist today' phrasing. 10-min task

new_findings.claim: QUICKSTART line 87 says the trace step shape is `{ node, inputs, outputs, durationUs }`. Shipped shape is `{ nodeCid, primitive, durationUs, inputs, outputs, error }`
new_findings.detail: Minor field-rename drift: `node` in doc vs `nodeCid` in ship. Shipped shape is richer (adds primitive, error). Still doc-vs-ship drift
new_findings.suggested_fix: Update QUICKSTART line 87 to reflect the richer shape. One-line doc fix

top_3_pass2_concerns: The 10-minute QUICKSTART does not survive a verbatim user walkthrough — three of the documented code snippets throw errors. This is the headline Phase-1 deliverable; a new user first-contact hits three confusing errors in the first 5 minutes.

The 'Adding schema validation' section documents an option that silently doesn't exist. This is worse than a runtime error — the user gets NO feedback that their validation rules aren't enforced, and writes above maxLength succeed.

SECURITY-POSTURE.md #8 gap means the historical record of the Engine::call fast-path compromise is NOT in the security doc. Commit 25d2893 claimed closure; the regression test exists; the doc narration is missing. Low bulk-risk but weak audit trail.
confidence: high — every finding verified by either executing the code path against a freshly-scaffolded project or reading the actual source and comparing to the doc text verbatim. Nothing trusted to SECURITY-POSTURE.md or CLAUDE.md claims
recommended_disposition: PASS-WITH-DOC-FIXES — the shipping product is SOUND. Rust/TS tests exercise real behavior; 527 pass / 0 fail / 17 honestly-ignored. The Rust engine correctly implements what the user actually needs. The blockers are DOCUMENTATION drift: QUICKSTART doesn't match the shipped API surface. Recommend: before Phase-1 public announcement, do a 60-minute doc-sweep fixing the 4 QUICKSTART drifts + missing #8 section + stale module-doc headers. Ship stays on 90eb694; the doc-sweep is a single PR.

cross_pass_summary.delta: Pass-2 found 3 critical + 1 major + 2 new-minor that pass-1 missed (the QUICKSTART drifts + the SECURITY-POSTURE #8 gap + stale module-doc headers).
